channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: GLP-1 Medications and the Risk of Yo-Yo Dieting Cycles
item.description: The article discusses the potential for weight-loss medications like Wegovy and Mounjaro to create a new cycle of yo-yo dieting. While these drugs, which mimic hormones to reduce appetite and increase...

item.title: Trump's Frustration Over Prolonged Iran Conflict
item.description: President Donald Trump reportedly unleashed a profanity-laden temper tantrum in the Oval Office over his failure to end the Iran war, according to an inside source. The incident, detailed in a Wall St...

item.title: Murphy Reid becomes youngest Fremantle player to win Glendinning-Allan Medal after derby triumph
item.description: Murphy Reid, 19, has made history by becoming the youngest player in Fremantle's AFL history to win the prestigious Glendinning-Allan Medal. The young star delivered a standout performance in Fremantl...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Deliberates on Suliman Carrim's Postponement Request
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, established to investigate political interference and corruption within South Africa's criminal justice system, is currently considering businessman Suliman Carrim'...

item.title: Canada’s Skilled Military Recruit Pathway Draws Few Invitations
item.description: Canada's immigration department, Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada (IRCC), has launched a new pathway under its Express Entry program targeting skilled foreign military personnel. The inaug...

item.title: Multiple-Vehicle Crash on Lagos-Ibadan Expressway Claims Motor Boy's Life
item.description: A tragic incident occurred on Kara Bridge along the Lagos-Ibadan Expressway when an articulated trailer carrying chemicals overturned, causing a chain-reaction collision involving three articulated tr...

item.title: Wildfire Smoke Continues to Impact Kelowna
item.description: Wildfire smoke has been persistently affecting the region of Kelowna, with residents experiencing poor air quality and health concerns. The article highlights the ongoing challenges posed by the smoke...

item.title: Record Number of Migrants Reach UK in Single Boat Amid Channel Crisis
item.description: A record 165 migrants have reached the UK in a single boat, breaking the previous Channel crossing record of 128 set in July. The Home Office confirmed the arrival, highlighting the dangers faced by t...

item.title: The Alibi Finale: Concluding Chapter for Stella and Vincent
item.description: The Alibi, a popular Philippine television series, is set to conclude its storyline with its final episode, marking the end of the journey for its main characters, Stella and Vincent. The finale, sche...

item.title: US imposes tariffs on 60 countries over forced labor allegations
item.description: The United States has imposed new tariffs of 10% and 12.5% on goods from 60 trading partners, including the EU and China, citing failures to curb imports linked to forced labor. This move follows the ...

item.title: Two Dawlah Bomb-Makers Arrested, Weapons Seized in Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: The Philippine Army has arrested two suspected members of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters-Dawlah Islamiya (BIFF-DI) group in Maguindanao del Sur, following a joint operation with local authori...

item.title: Pakistan and Tanzania Strengthen Trade and Diplomatic Ties
item.description: Outgoing Pakistan High Commissioner Siraj Ahmad Khan highlighted key achievements during his tenure, emphasizing strengthened trade relations, closer diplomatic engagement, and growing people-to-peopl...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi accuses government of misleading on pellet gun use during protests; HC rejects NIA inquiry
item.description: Rahul Gandhi, the Leader of the Opposition, accused the Union government of misleading the country by denying that security personnel used pellet guns against protesters during their march to Parliame...

item.title: Quebec OB-GYN Association Challenges Pay Gap in Legal Action
item.description: The Quebec Obstetricians and Gynecologists Association has filed a legal challenge against the Federation of Medical Specialists, alleging a persistent gender-related pay gap. Despite overall increase...

item.title: Jon Bon Jovi Reschedules MSG Show After Vocal Issues During Performance
item.description: Jon Bon Jovi abruptly ended his Madison Square Garden concert on July 23, 2026, due to vocal issues, informing fans he would reschedule the show. The incident occurred two weeks into his 'Bon Jovi For...

item.title: Drowning Risks in South Africa: Focus on Rivers, Dams, and Pools
item.description: South Africa faces a significant drowning crisis, with inland waterways like rivers and dams being major contributors to fatalities. The National Sea Rescue Institute (NSRI) reports over 1,500 annual ...

item.title: Cross-Generational Voices Discuss Nuclear Disarmament and Anti-War Strategies in Japan-Korea-US Policy
item.description: The 'Nuclear Bombs Never Again' webinar, scheduled for August 4th at 5 pm Pacific Time (8 pm Eastern Time / 9 am Japan Standard Time), will bring together cross-generational voices from Japan, South K...

item.title: Man Jailed for Life After Shaking Four-Week-Old Son in Alcohol-Fueled Outburst
item.description: Tony Bartlett, 39, was sentenced to life imprisonment with a minimum term of 18 years after violently shaking his four-week-old son Atticus to death. The incident occurred on July 16, 2022, when Bartl...

item.title: Madlanga Commission Deliberates on Suliman Carrim's Testimony Delay
item.description: The Madlanga Commission is reviewing whether North West businessman Suliman Carrim should be granted an indefinite postponement from testifying due to alleged health issues. Evidence presented suggest...

item.title: Conservative Criticizes Todd Blanche's Appointment as Unfit for Attorney General Role
